stories.title: Ukraine war briefing: EU moves to unlock €90bn loan as Orbán exits
stories.summary: Macron says ‘reasonably optimistic’ loan with go through after election defeat of pro-Russian Hungarian PM. What we know on day 1,518 European Union countries will on Wednesday move to unlock a €90bn loan for Ukraine as the defeated Viktor Orbán exits power in Hungary. The now-caretaker Hungarian prime minister, friendly to Vladimir Putin, had been blocking the loan but will soon be replaced by Peter Magyar, who won a sweeping electoral mandate. Magyar has promised smoother relations with Brusse

stories.title: Orbán’s EU fixer faces becoming Hungary’s ‘fall guy’
stories.summary: Orbán’s EU fixer faces becoming Hungary’s ‘fall guy’ Ambassador Bálint Ódor’s knowledge of the EU’s inner workings helped the outgoing government forcefully make its points for years. But his time in Brussels looks like it’s coming to an end. By GABRIEL GAVIN Illustration by Natália Delgado/POLITICO Under Viktor Orbán, Hungary needed someone in Brussels who could aggressively defend his government’s belligerent anti-EU stance while quietly working with o

stories.title: From Attila the Hun to Donald J. Trump: A brief history of popes battling world leaders
stories.summary: Donald Trump wasn’t the first, and he likely won’t be the last. The U.S. president’s simmering feud with Pope Leo XIV is just the latest in a millennia-spanning tradition of world leaders beefing with the head of the Catholic Church. War in Iran joins the decline of the Roman Empire and the French Revolution as historic flashpoints that have triggered dust-ups between pontiffs and some of civilization’s most iconic political figures. The latest clash has its origin in the

stories.title: EU foreign ministers meet to discuss Ukraine, Russia and the Middle East – Europe live
stories.summary: Representatives from EU countries are meeting in Luxembourg today EU foreign ministers are meeting in Luxembourg this morning to discuss the latest on Ukraine, Russia and the Middle East. The ministers are awaiting an update on the Druzbha pipeline carrying Russian oil to Hungary and Slovakia, which is understood to be close to being operational against after months of bitter disputes between Ukraine and the two countries. “Now this country [has recently] had the elections and will [soon] have a

stories.title: Hungary’s Magyar eyes swift political deal to unfreeze EU funds after Orbán years – as it happened
stories.summary: New Hungarian leader says he could sign a political agreement with the EU in mid-May The commission also got asked about the Italian proposals for a “wild west-style bounties” that could be paid to Italian lawyers if they successfully convince their immigrant clients to return home. Our Rome correspondent Angela Giuffrida reported on the controversial proposal over the weekend: Continue reading...

stories.title: Ukraine hits Russian Black Sea port city, 1 dead
stories.summary: Russian authorities said one man was killed in a Ukrainian drone raid on city of Tuapse. Meanwhile, Russia arrested a German woman in an alleged bomb plot.

stories.title: Romania plunges back into political turmoil as Social Democrats move to topple PM
stories.summary: Romania’s Social Democrats have moved to oust Prime Minister Ilie Bolojan, triggering a new political crisis in the turmoil-weary country. The party, which governs in coalition with Bolojan’s liberals, voted overwhelmingly on Monday to withdraw its political support for the prime minister. The Social Democrats command the largest share of votes in the Romanian parliament. Party leader Sorin Grindeanu accused Bolojan, who presides over the center-right National Liberal Party, of leading an a

stories.title: Tuesday briefing: What it might take for lasting peace between the US and Iran
stories.summary: In today’s newsletter: Our diplomatic editor on whether permanent peace is possible – or whether there will be a new escalation in the conflict Good morning. The Gulf is stuck in limbo between war and peace. Despite a ceasefire deal between the US and Iran, both sides have ramped up threats once again. A lasting end to the violence feels possible, but so does a renewed round of fighting – and more death, destruction and economic pain. JD Vance, the US vice-president, is expected to fly to Pakist

stories.title: Scarborn (Kos) review – rumbustuous period epic stirs up trouble in 18th-century Poland
stories.summary: When a veteran of the American war of independence returns to his native country with popular rule on his mind dangerous passions ignite This Polish historical drama is an odd duck of a feature but there’s definitely a cinematic flair to proceedings. For a start, a lot of it unfolds over one evening in a candlelit setting, so you may have to screw up your eyes in order to make out what’s going on. My advice is to then surrender to its strange tonality and weird flat stretches, because the ending

stories.title: Rat poison found in baby food, extortion suspected
stories.summary: Rat poison has been found in HiPP baby food jars across Austria, Czechia and Slovakia in what authorities suspect is a blackmail attempt.

stories.title: From soldier to developer: How German troops are test building their own drones
stories.summary: During the "Grand Quadriga 2026" exercise, the German army has been demonstrating the transformation of modern warfare, where drones, data and speed are increasingly decisive.

stories.title: 'If Druzhba is operational, Zelenskyy should open it,' Magyar says about pipeline
stories.summary: "He should not go down that street," incoming Hungarian PM Péter Magyar said when asked about new potential Ukrainian demands to reopen the Druzhba pipeline. Brussels ready for Hungary to lift veto if oil flows resume.

stories.title: Fluort is hívták a Rendszerbontó Nagykoncertre, elmagyarázta, miért nem vállalta a fellépést
stories.summary: Beszélt arról is, hogyan élte meg a választás eredményét.

stories.title: Macron szerint a magyar kormányváltás egész Európának egy új korszak kezdetét jelenti
stories.summary: Macron állítása szerint „észszerűen optimista” a 90 milliárd eurós hitel ügyében.

stories.title: A tábornok, akiből honvédelmi miniszter alakú lőlap lett
stories.summary: A Tisza Párt első sztárigazolása egyből a kormánymédia célkeresztjébe került, így a közvélemény mindent megtudhatott zsírleszívásáról és hálószobájáról, pedig pár héttel korábban még a kormányzati nyilvánosságban hirdette az oroszbarát narratívát a háborúról. A Tisza Párt az utolsó hetekben hátrébb is vonta a kampány frontvonalából.

stories.title: Pogány Induló már nem akar senkinek megfelelni
stories.summary: Felemás kísérlet vagy félkész mestermű? Pogány Induló Valamit majd kitalálok című harmadik lemeze megosztó lett, de ez egyben karrierjének eddigi legsokszínűbb vállalkozása.

stories.title: Megvan az Apple új vezetője, Tim Cook utódja
stories.summary: A hardverért felelős John Ternus veszi át Tim Cook vezérigazgatói posztját ősztől. Cook az igazgatótanács elnöke lesz.

stories.title: Kapitány István meglátogatta Nagy Mártont és Szijjártó Pétert
stories.summary: Kapitány Orbán Anitával és Kármán Andrással közösen tárgyalt az átadás-átvételi folyamatok elindításáról.

stories.title: Ókovács Szilveszter Magyar Péternek: Mi van, ha nem olvasok Telexet?
stories.summary: Az Operaház igazgatója szerint már adtak egy ajánlatot a kormánynak az október 23-i megemlékezés megtartására.

stories.title: Senki nem akart Magyarországon krumplit enni, súlyos propagandára volt szükség, hogy burgonyaimádókká váljunk
stories.summary: Annyira fontos a magyarok számára a krumpli, hogy elképzelni is nehéz, mennyire tiltakozott a fogyasztása ellen a nép még kétszáz évvel ezelőtt is.

stories.title: Mi történt a politikatudomány szerint Magyarországon 2026. április 12-én?
stories.summary: Elsöprő győzelmet ért el a Tisza a Fidesszel szemben április 12-én, de mi történt valójában? Hogyan viszonyult a demokrácia és a Fidesz által kiépített 16 éves rezsim a hazai politikai rendszerhez? Mit mond a szakirodalom és legfőképpen: mi az a választási forradalom?

stories.title: Nem garázsokkal és parkolóházakkal kellene megoldani Budapest parkolási problémáit
stories.summary: A magánparkolók támogatása rengeteg közpénzbe kerül, minimális a hatása, és a közterületek humanizálását is hátráltatja.

stories.title: Mennyit számított a választáson a jelöltek személye?
stories.summary: Öt olyan választókerületet találtunk, ahol nem annak a pártnak a jelöltje nyert, amelyik listán több szavazatot kapott. Az ál-Magyar Péter és a Mi Hazánk jó eséllyel be tudott kavarni az egyéni eredményeknél, Hadházy nem.

stories.title: A 444 szerint megkezdődött a Fideszben az Orbán Viktor-mentő hadművelet
stories.summary: A lap a távozó miniszterelnök közvetlen környezetéből származó értesülései szerint többen Orbán Balázst tartják felelősnek az eredményért.

stories.title: Júniusig nem kell szolidaritási hozzájárulást fizetnie Budapestnek
stories.summary: Az Államkincstár mentesítette a fővárost, és Szolnok is haladékot kapott.

stories.title: GKI: Sokkal több benzint tudunk venni ma a fizetésünkből, mint 2010-ben
stories.summary: Nem is annyira az olaj ára, inkább a magyar szabályozás dobálta azt az elmúlt másfél évtizedben, hogy egy átlagbért kereső magyar mennyi üzemanyagot tud venni a fizetéséből.

stories.title: Ügyészségi hatáskörbe vonta a legfőbb ügyész a Magyar Nemzeti Bankot érintő nyomozást
stories.summary: A Központi Nyomozó Főügyészség nyomoz tovább. Az ügyészség részletesen levezette, mi ennek az oka, azt írták, a büntetőeljárási törvény a nyomozást két részre osztja: felderítési és vizsgálati szakaszra, ahol a választóvonalat a gyanúsítotti kihallgatás jelenti. Eső után köpönyeg, reagált Magyar Péter a döntésre.

stories.title: Áll a bál a Muzsika TV körül: egy volt műsorvezető szerint a jogdíj felét oda kellett adni, ha valaki képernyőn akart maradni
stories.summary: A csatorna volt főszerkesztője szerint a vádak légből kapottak, egyesek a sikertelenséget magyarázzák így.

stories.title: Elbukta a kormány a pert, uniós jogot sért a melegeket pedofilokkal összemosó törvény
stories.summary: Ha a magyar állam nem változtat a jogszabályon, újabb per jöhet – már pénzbüntetés megállapításáról.

stories.title: Egy nagy lépéssel közelebb kerültünk ahhoz, hogy lássuk a Magyar Péter által elképzelt új államot
stories.summary: Megtartotta első frakcióülése utáni tájékoztatóját a Tisza Párt, ahol szó esett régi botrányok kivizsgálásáról, új államszerkezetről, az arrogancia és a luxizás következményeiről, és a kemény külpolitikai üzenetek se maradtak el.

stories.title: A tánca után a betegközpontú magyar egészségügy reformjával szerezne újra világhírt Hegedűs Zsolt
stories.summary: A John Travolta-i képességeiről is ismert ortopéd szakorvos a brit rendszer legjobb elemeit honosítaná meg a Tisza-kormány egészségügyi minisztereként.

stories.title: Orbán mindig óriási csatát vizionálva indult az EU-csúcsokra, az utolsót most csendben elsunnyogja
stories.summary: Rendes búcsú nélkül távozik az uniós állam- és kormányfők testületéből a magát utcai harcosként meghatározó Orbán. Nem vesz részt a hét végi informális EU-csúcson, így biztos nem kell beszélnie Szijjártó és Lavrov telefonjairól sem a többi vezetővel. Egy bekeretezett fotót azért küldenek neki, a vétói és blokkolásai pedig akár hosszú távon is kihathatnak az uniós döntéshozatalra.

stories.title: Pilóta akart lenni, a Shell alelnökeként ért a csúcsra a Tisza-kormány új gazdasági és energetikai minisztere
stories.summary: Kapitány István 1987-ben a Sugár Üzletközpont üzletvezető-helyetteseként sikeresen megnyugtatott egy reklamáló vevőt, aki történetesen a Shell magyarországi leányvállalatának akkori vezetője volt. Így kezdődött 37 éves pályafutása az olajipari óriásnál.

stories.title: Vihart kavart egy doppingügy, de végül elindulhat a Magyar Körön az MBH Bank bringacsapata
stories.summary: A Tour de Hongrie főszervezője egy doppingeset miatt kollektív büntetésként először visszavonta a klub meghívását, aztán meggondolta magát.

stories.title: Orbán Balázs kielemezte a Tisza győzelmét és leendő kormányzását, de a Fidesz vesztes kampányát nem említette
stories.summary: Szerinte más országokban a választópolgároknak hamar elege lett a centristák „se hal, se hús” megoldásaiból, ekkorra kell készen állnia egy megújult patrióta oldalnak.

stories.title: Büszke paraszt a Tisza Párt leendő agrár- és élelmiszer-minisztere, Bóna Szabolcs
stories.summary: Konzervatív jobboldalinak vallja magát Bóna Szabolcs, aki volt az év állattartója mint a Rábapordányi Mezőgazdasági Zrt. elnök-igazgatója, most pedig a kormány agrár- és élelmiszer-minisztere lesz belőle. Bóna rendszerszintű átalakítást tervez az ágazatban, és az EU-val is szembeszállna a magyar gazdák érdekében.

stories.title: Gajda Attila a pedofilvádakról: Nem lehetünk következmények nélküli ország
stories.summary: A Tisza megválasztott szegedi képviselője reagált a kormányközeli sajtóban megjelent pedofilvádakra. A választások előtt a Délmagyar arról cikkezett, hogy Gajdával szemben kiskorú elleni szexuális bűncselekmény miatt nyomoztak.

stories.title: A román szociáldemokraták megvonták a bizalmat a miniszterelnöktől, kormányválság jöhet
stories.summary: A legnagyobb kormánypárt kihátrált az egyre népszerűtlenebb miniszterelnök mögül.

stories.title: Szörnyű dolog 14 évesen elveszíteni egy anyát – az erről szóló regény mégis reményteli
stories.summary: Elena Fischer német díjra jelölt könyvében egy magyar származású család próbálja összekapni magát a tragédiából.

stories.title: Austria: Police find rat poison in recalled baby food jar
stories.summary: The Germany-based HiPP had recalled the jars, suggesting they were tampered with. The contaminated jars were also found in the Czech Republic and Slovakia.

stories.title: Péter Magyar’s real coup was winning over loyal Orbán voters – not preaching to the converted
stories.summary: He is no progressive but unlike the old opposition, the Tisza leader listened to Orbán’s rural base The international audience observing the Hungarian election result is likely to settle on a view that feels familiar. That this election was about east v west or that it was a “youthquake”, a win secured by the unprecedented participation of young voters. These narratives have some truth to them, of course, but, especially for those interested in fighting back against regimes such as Viktor Orbán’

stories.title: The problem with Europe’s Big Tech breakup: It’s still hooked
stories.summary: The problem with Europe’s Big Tech breakup: It’s still hooked Thanks to Trump, EU governments are trying to break their overreliance on U.S. technology. It won’t be cheap or easy. By MATHIEU POLLET and ANOUK SCHLUNG in AMSTERDAM Illustration by Natália Delgado/ POLITICO Inside Amsterdam’s red-brick city hall, Alexander Scholtes, the official who spearheaded the Dutch capital’s push to break up with U.S. technology, looked down at his tablet. stories.summary: Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi approved new rules expanding Japan's ability to export advanced defense equipment, a major shift as Tokyo boosts defense spending and its arms industry.

stories.title: 7 reasons Starmer can’t shake off the Mandelson vetting saga
stories.summary: LONDON — Peter Mandelson is the curse that never ends for Keir Starmer. Britain’s prime minister accused his own foreign ministry Monday of deliberately concealing the fact that vetting officials recommended Mandelson should be denied full security clearance as U.K. ambassador to Washington. stories.title: Pennsylvania court strikes down ban on use of Medicaid funds for abortions
stories.summary: Victory for abortion rights groups as court finds state’s constitution guarantees a right to abortion A Pennsylvania court on Monday said that the state’s constitution guarantees a right to abortion while striking down a decades-long law banning the use of state Medicaid funds to cover abortion costs. The ruling by a divided seven-judge panel of the appellate-level commonwealth court is a major victory for Planned Parenthood and abortion clinic operators who first sued Pennsylvania over its Medi

stories.title: Myanmar military regime widens sanitary towel ban, claiming rebels use them for first aid
stories.summary: Activists say clamp down on period products to target insurgents is gender-based violence and violates rights Myanmar’s military regime is expanding its ban on the distribution of period products, claiming they are being used to treat wounded resistance fighters, according to local activists. The south-east Asian country has been locked in civil war since 2021 , when the military usurped the democratic government and launched a violent crackdown on dissidents. stories.title: Iran war: Talks in doubt as leaders issue threats
stories.summary: While the talks remain in limbo, the United States and Iran have been trading threats. Separately, media reports suggest US Vice President JD Vance is set to travel to Pakistan for ceasefire negotiations. DW has more.

stories.title: Almost 43,000 migrants register in first three days of Spain's regularisation amnesty
stories.summary: Spain's position sharply differs from prevailing attitudes on immigration in Europe, where many governments have been trying to curb arrivals and step up deportations.

stories.title: Why early action matters for cardiovascular health
stories.summary: Europe has an opportunity to address avoidable cardiovascular disease (CVD) risk and ease the burden of chronic and infectious diseases on national health systems. However, this requires a shift in EU policy from late-stage treatment to earlier detection and timely prevention of CVD. Health innovation now enables earlier identification of individuals at risk of developing CVD, making it possible to implement preventive interventions that slow or prevent disease progression, while strengthening h

stories.title: Arrests fuel fears among Madagascar’s gen Z protesters that new regime no better than one they overthrew
stories.summary: Jubilation is turning to disenchantment as young activists arrested after protest calling for election date to be set The arrest of several protesters in Madagascar has increased fears among young people that the military regime that took power last year after huge Gen Z demonstrations will be no better than the government it overthrew. Four Gen Z activists, Herizo Andriamanantena, Miora Rakotomalala, Dina Randrianarisoa and Nomena Ratsihorimanana, were arrested on 12 April, one of their lawyers

stories.title: Tim Cook to step down as Apple chief as John Ternus named replacement
stories.summary: Cook, who will stay on as executive chair, praises head of hardware engineering, who will take over on 1 September Apple announced on Monday that it had named a replacement for Tim Cook as CEO after nearly 15 years, with head of hardware engineering John Ternus succeeding him on 1 September. Cook will stay at the company in the role of executive chair. “It has been the greatest privilege of my life to be the CEO of Apple and to have been trusted to lead such an extraordinary company. I love Appl

stories.title: Fitness influencer drowns during swimming portion of Ironman Texas
stories.summary: Mara Flavia Souza Araujo drowns early on Saturday Rescue crews locate body after athlete vanished A Brazilian fitness influencer has died after getting into difficulty during the swimming portion of an ironman event in Texas. Mara Flavia Souza Araujo was reported as a “lost swimmer” around 7.30am at the Ironman Texas in Lake Woodlands near Houston on Saturday. According to KPRC 2 News , safety crews could not immediately locate Araujo. The 38-year-old’s body was discovered around 90 minutes late

stories.title: Who is John Ternus, Apple’s next CEO?
stories.summary: Hardware engineering executive is a longtime Apple insider, indicating company will continue strategy that has led to record profits Apple has announced longtime company veteran John Ternus as the next CEO of the company, succeeding current CEO Tim Cook, who is set to transition to executive chair of Apple’s board of directors later this year. Ternus’s term as CEO will begin on 1 September. The hardware engineering executive is a longtime Apple insider, indicating the company will stay the cours

stories.title: Picasso’s Guernica is the ultimate emblem of the horrors of war. It has no place in Spain's partisan squabbles | María Ramírez
stories.summary: Forty years after the 1937 masterpiece returned to Madrid from its Franco-era exile in New York, it is again embroiled in politics Every September, Spain celebrates one of the most symbolic moments of its transition to democracy. This year will mark 45 years since an Iberia commercial flight from New York landed in Madrid with its pilot announcing to the surprised passengers that they had just travelled with one of the country’s most famous exiles: Pablo Picasso’s Guernica. After more than four

stories.title: Israeli soldiers using sexual assault to force Palestinians out of West Bank, report says
stories.summary: Experts say attacks, also carried out by settlers, are leading girls to quit school and enter early marriages Israeli soldiers and settlers are using gendered violence and sexual assault and harassment to force Palestinians from their homes in the occupied West Bank, human rights and legal experts say. stories.summary: Alarm caused by posts of Alex Karp, tech firm’s CEO, championing US military dominance and of AI weapons The US spy tech company Palantir published a manifesto extolling the benefits of American power and implying some cultures are inferior to others – in what MPs have called “a parody of a RoboCop film” and “the ramblings of a supervillain”. “Some cultures have produced vital advances; others remain dysfunctional and regressive,” wrote Palantir in a 22-point post on X over the weekend, which al

stories.title: Cattle breed whose ancestors lived with Celts added to priority at-risk list
stories.summary: UK’s Rare Breeds Survival Trust says calf numbers of white park cattle last year were less than two-thirds of 2022 level An ancient breed of cattle whose ancestors are thought to have accompanied the Celts as they were pushed to Britain’s fringes by the Romans has been designated as urgently at risk by a UK conservation charity. stories.title: JD Vance to lead US delegation in Pakistan if Iran agrees to talks

stories.title: Humanoid robots show rapid advances as they race past humans in Beijing half marathon

stories.summary: Vincent Bollore has vowed to find new authors for publisher Grasset after 170 left, alleging political interference. French President Macron has weighed in on the affair, which has shaken France's cultural scene.

stories.title: Climate change impacts India's harvest festivals
stories.summary: As people across India celebrate traditional agrarian spring festivals, climate change has become an unwanted guest at the table. How are communities rising to the pressure on harvests, water, and rural life?

stories.title: Life in Donbas: 'If we give up, there will be nothing left'
stories.summary: The Russian army is advancing on the Ukrainian town of Kostiantynivka, with the aim of occupying the entire Donbas region. Yet people continue to live and fight in the area, as DW's Hanna Sokolova-Stekh found out.

stories.title: Bayern Munich beat Stuttgart to clinch Bundesliga title
stories.summary: Bayern Munich are German league champions for a record-extending 35th time after beating Stuttgart 4-2. They have now won 13 of the past 14 Bundesliga titles.

stories.title: The German refugees who found shelter in Yugoslavia
stories.summary: After the Nazis seized power in 1933, many Germans escaped by fleeing abroad. Some ended up in what was then Yugoslavia. German historian Marie Janine Calic tells their story.

stories.title: China hosts humanoid robots in Beijing half marathon
stories.summary: China has tested humanoid robots in a real race setting in Beijing. The event highlights advances in endurance, balance and AI behaviour. Engineers see it as a step towards wider use.

stories.title: Strait of Hormuz standoff reignites as IRGC defies Iran’s diplomats
stories.summary: The Strait of Hormuz has been blocked again by Iran, as the IRGC’s hardline stance appears to prevail in Tehran. US Vice President JD Vance will lead the US delegation for talks in Pakistan on Monday, after President Trump said he would not attend.

stories.title: Two police officers suspended after fleeing Kyiv shooting scene
stories.summary: An inquiry was opened after a video showed officers fleeing in panic from Saturday’s shooting, which left six dead and many injured. The patrol police chief has resigned.

stories.title: 'They told me he was dead': Children born near army base learn truth about UK soldier dads
stories.summary: A DNA and legal project has identified the fathers of 20 children born near a military base in Kenya.

stories.title: Iran pulls out of Islamabad peace talks after US seizes cargo ship
stories.summary: Tehran’s decision comes after the US Navy halted an Iranian-flagged cargo vessel near the Strait of Hormuz on Sunday, in what was the first interception since Washington instated its naval blockade of Iranian ports.

stories.title: Carney says Canada must cut reliance on US as tariffs hit economy
stories.summary: The Canadian leader stressed that his country cannot hinge its future on hope that the US will return to the partner it formerly was to his country and that active steps must be taken to protect Ottawa from the disruptions caused by the Trump administration.

stories.title: Oil prices climb as US-Iran stand-off keeps Strait of Hormuz in limbo
stories.summary: Oil prices climbed more than 5% while European markets fell on Monday morning as a stand-off between Iran and the US prevented tankers from using the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: New Zealand declares state of emergency in Wellington as floods hit
stories.summary: Footage online shows vehicles submerged, trees uprooted and houses hit by landslides.

stories.title: Kites fill the sky as Berck-sur-Mer launches 39th international festival
stories.summary: The 39th International Kite Festival in Berck-sur-Mer fills the sky with giant creations and hosts the World Kite Cup, drawing huge international crowds.

stories.title: Major fire in Malaysia destroys hundreds of homes, displaces thousands
stories.summary: A massive fire in Sandakan, Malaysia, destroyed about 1,000 stilt homes and displaced more than 9,000 people, with survivors now sheltering in temporary camps.

stories.title: Louisiana gunman kills eight children in suspected domestic mass shooting
stories.summary: A man in Louisiana fatally shot eight children, including seven of his own, in an attack on his family on Sunday morning in Louisiana.

stories.title: Obama and Mamdani sing with kids during first meeting in New York
stories.summary: Barack Obama joined New York mayor Zohran Mamdani at a child care centre, reading to preschoolers and backing his affordability agenda for families.

stories.title: Moment US navy fires on and seizes Iranian-flagged cargo ship
stories.summary: US military footage shows a destroyer firing warning shots before boarding and seizing an Iranian-flagged cargo ship in the Gulf of Oman after it tried to evade a naval blockade, amid heightened tensions near the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: US releases video of forces seizing Iranian ship
stories.summary: The US has intercepted an Iranian-flagged cargo ship in the Gulf as part of its naval blockade, Donald Trump has said.

stories.title: Rutte dismisses speculation about US leaving NATO
stories.summary: NATO chief Mark Rutte said he understands Donald Trump's frustration with NATO. He also called on Europe to boost its defense industry.

stories.title: Europe looks to secure shipping in Strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: A coalition of nonbelligerent states led by France and the UK has said it is ready to lead an international mission to restore navigation in the Strait of Hormuz. But that won't be deployed before the Iran war is over.

stories.title: Record fuel prices: How are governments responding?
stories.summary: In Germany, a liter of diesel costs up to 40% more than before the Iran war and other countries have seen even bigger price jumps. What are governments around the world doing to combat record fuel prices?

stories.title: The German who recovered from a stroke to seal Olympic first
stories.summary: Kathrin Marchand was an elite rower for years before becoming a doctor. But a stroke at the age of 30 changed everything. Despite the challenges, she has now achieved something no other Olympian ever has.

stories.title: Hannover Messe 2026: Brazilian flair awaits visitors
stories.summary: With war in Iran on hold, the world's largest industrial trade fair opens in Hannover. And Brazil, this year's partner country, is pulling out all stops to convince visitors it's a reliable technology leader.

stories.title: Olive harvesting at the Colosseum: a plantation in Roman ruins in the heart of the city
stories.summary: There are about 2,200 farms within the city limits of Italy’s capital alone, making it the largest agricultural municipality in Europe.

stories.title: Pope Leo XIV lands in Angola, says it is 'not in my interest at all' to debate Trump
stories.summary: After visiting Cameroon, Angola is the third leg of Pope Leo XIV's 11-day tour of Africa. People hope for appeals for peace and for him to tackle the economic woes of the oil- and rare-earth-rich nation.

stories.title: Iran says deal still 'far off' as Hormuz Strait remains shut
stories.summary: Amid stalled US-Iran negotiations, Iranian officials warning the crucial shipping lane will remain shut until the US lifts its blockade of Iranian ports.

stories.title: North Korea conducts multiple ballistic missile tests
stories.summary: North Korea fired several short-range ballistic missiles into the East Sea on Sunday, the latest in a series of recent weapons tests that analysts say signal Pyongyang's rejection of South Korea's attempts to ease tensions.

stories.title: Trump signs order to fast-track research on psychedelic drugs
stories.summary: Trump signed an executive order Saturday to ease research restrictions on psychedelic drugs like psilocybin and LSD, aiming to accelerate studies into their potential for treating mental health conditions.

stories.title: About 250 missing after boat carrying Rohingya refugees capsizes in Andaman Sea
stories.summary: Trawler set off from Bangladesh and reportedly capsized due to heavy winds, rough seas and overcrowding About 250 people are missing after a boat carrying Rohingya refugees and Bangladeshi nationals capsized in the Andaman Sea, according to the UN’s refugee and migration agencies. The agencies said the trawler carrying more than 250 men, women and children reportedly sank due to harsh weather and overcrowding. It had departed from Teknaf in southern Bangladesh and was bound for Malaysia. Continu

stories.title: More than half of Britons support rejoining EU 10 years on from Brexit vote
stories.summary: Experts say Labour’s ‘halfway house’ approach risks losing support from progressives and ‘red wall’ voters Support for rejoining the EU rather than simply rejoining the single market is growing among British voters, with more than 80% of Labour, Liberal Democrat and Green party supporters favouring this option, according to research mapping voter attitudes 10 years after the Brexit referendum. Labour’s “muted” approach to the issue means it risks losing support among progressive voters and in “r

stories.title: Venezuela’s Machado to hold Madrid rally as opposition frozen out after Maduro capture
stories.summary: Exiled leader to revive push for change amid US backing of Delcy Rodríguez and delays to democratic transition Venezuela’s opposition leader, María Corina Machado, will seek to revive her push for political change with a rally in Madrid on Saturday, having found herself sidelined by Donald Trump after the abduction of the president Nicolás Maduro. “Venezuela will be free,” the Nobel peace prize winner insisted in an interview on the eve of this weekend’s demonstration in the Puerta del Sol squar

stories.title: Several killed in Russian strikes across Ukraine, many more injured
stories.summary: A 12-year-old was among those killed as Russian missile and drone attacks hit Ukrainian cities of Kyiv, Odesa and Dnipro.

stories.title: Will Bulgaria's election change the country's course?
stories.summary: Bulgarians will vote in their eighth parliamentary election in five years on April 19. Former President Rumen Radev is projected to win. Radev, who has a record of pro-Russian stances, is pledging to fight corruption.

stories.title: UK woman fears being stranded in Spain over new Home Office border rules
stories.summary: Natasha Cochrane de la Rosa was refused boarding on flight to London because she was not aware of the rule change A British woman has told how she fears being stranded in Spain for months after being refused boarding a on flight back home to London because she was not aware of new Home Office border rules. Natasha Cochrane de la Rosa, 26, was born in the UK to a British father and a Spanish mother, but because of archaic laws she was not entitled automatically to British citizenship because her

stories.title: Home Office ‘red flag’ error leaves German mother separated from toddler in UK
stories.summary: Liza Tobay was told settled status had been ‘red flagged’ when she tried to fly home from Germany to Scotland A German woman has been separated from her two-year-old daughter in Edinburgh after a Home Office mistake left her stranded in Dusseldorf earlier this week. Liza Tobay, who has lived in the UK for 15 years, had taken her oldest child, a six-year-old boy, to visit his grandfather and some other relatives over Easter when confronted with what she said appeared to be “a serious administrati

stories.title: Weather tracker: hail covers parts of Tunisia and Algeria like snow
stories.summary: Accumulations of up to 3cm deep reported as severe thunderstorms also bring heavy downpours to central Italy Severe thunderstorms have affected the Mediterranean this week. On Monday, a surface low-pressure system in the Mediterranean in conjunction with an upper air cut-off low, led to thunderstorms over north Africa. Their intensity was aided by the hot precursor conditions. Algeria and Tunisia were notably affected by the thunderstorms, with some hail accumulation layers as a result. When so

stories.title: Kenyan firm sacks more than 1,000 workers after losing Meta contract
stories.summary: Meta paused work with Sama last month after allegations about staff viewing private scenes filmed by smart glasses More than 1,000 low-paid workers in Kenya have been abruptly sacked by an outsourcing company contracted by Meta, in what activists said was a shocking move exposing the precariousness of tech jobs in the global south. Sama, a company based in Nairobi to which Meta outsourced content moderation and AI training work, announced on Thursday that the workers were being laid off after Me

stories.title: India fails to pass bill to boost women’s representation after delimitation row
stories.summary: Opposition accuses Narendra Modi government of using quotas as cover for redrawing electoral map The Indian government has failed to pass a bill to increase female representation in parliament after being accused of using the plan as a guise to redraw the country’s electoral map. It was the first time in 12 years in power that a constitutional amendment proposed by Narendra Modi’s Bharatiya Janata party (BJP) government was not passed by parliament. Continue reading...
